name: Eggnog Pie
recipeIngredient: 2 to 4 Tbsp brandy or rum, to taste
Freshly grated nutmeg if possible, otherwise ground nutmeg
recipeInstructions:
Prepare the pie shell:
Combine the flour, sugar and salt in a small bowl. Cut in the shortening using a pastry blender until the mixture resembles coarse cornmeal. Gradually add about 1/4 cup of ice water, mixing with your hands to form a dough that holds together but stays cold. Add more water if needed.
Roll out the dough to about 1/4 inch thick and fold into quarters. Place in a 9-inch pie pan. Fold the edges under and press them down using your thumb and forefinger. Prick the bottom and sides with a fork. Bake at 400°F for 15 minutes or until golden brown, then cool to room temperature.
Make the filling:
Sprinkle gelatin over half a cup of milk and let it soften. In the top portion of a double boiler combine cornstarch, salt, and 3 tablespoons sugar with the rest of the milk and beaten egg yolks. Cook over simmering water until thickened, stirring constantly for 10 minutes.
Stir in the gelatin mixture into the thickened milk mixture. Return to heat and cook for an additional 5 minutes while stirring. Remove from heat, cover, and refrigerate until chilled.
Beat egg whites until frothy and add remaining sugar, beating until stiff. Add brandy or rum (or extracts) to the chilled milk mixture. Fold in beaten egg whites and pour into the cooled pie shell. Sprinkle with nutmeg. Chill for at least 1 hour before serving.
YIELD: 8 servings
